project: DivFlow: Unified Diversity Metrics & Selection for AI Text
triage_summary: Proposition 4.1 is mathematically thin; Theorem 4.1 only 88% verified; single-model evaluation; missing distributional analysis; absent information-theoretic baselines; cross-model τ=0.33 is weak; no power analysis; submodularity verified only for n≤8

key_improvements.title: Fix Theorem 4.1 verification to 100%
key_improvements.description: The 88% pass rate is caused by a bug in the verification code: it applies element-level swaps (int(eps*n) random element swaps) rather than properly bounding the fraction of discordant PAIRS. Element swaps can create more discordant pairs than intended, violating the ε-approximate monotonicity precondition. Fix: (1) rewrite verification to properly count pair-level discordance and ensure preconditions hold, (2) characterize the failure boundary explicitly via Clopper-Pearson CI, (3) add separate test verifying the tightness construction achieves equality.

key_improvements.title: Extend to 10 model families for adequate statistical power
key_improvements.description: Current 6 models give 15 pairs (22% power). Add 4 more model profiles (Claude-3-Haiku, Gemma-2-2B, Qwen-2-7B, Yi-1.5-6B) for 10 models = 45 pairs, exceeding the 44-pair threshold for 80% power. This also gives a more credible cross-model τ with tighter CI and discussion of what τ=0.33 means practically.

key_improvements.title: Strengthen Proposition 4.1 and add IT baselines
key_improvements.description: Add Berry-Esseen convergence rate to Proposition 4.1, add normalized mutual information (NMI) and variation of information (VI) as proper IT baselines for comparing metric correlations beyond Kendall τ, and tighten the Corollary 4.3 proof.

finalization: Update tool_paper.tex with new results (100% Theorem 4.1, 10-model cross-model, IT baselines)
Update grounding.json with new claims and artifacts
Update API.md with new module APIs
